steps.title: Convert line r to slope-intercept form
steps.content:
To find the slope of line $r$, convert $6x - 5y = 9$ to slope-intercept form:
$$6x - 5y = 9$$
$$-5y = -6x + 9$$
$$y = \frac{6}{5}x - \frac{9}{5}$$
The slope of line $r$ is $\frac{6}{5}$.

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines property
steps.content: Parallel lines have equal slopes. Since line $s$ is parallel to line $r$, the slope of line $s$ equals the slope of line $r$, which is $\frac{6}{5}$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
